description:
GOAL: Inline non-tail calls — `Term::Call(F, args) -> Cont{K, captured}` where
F is inlinable. F's Return becomes a TailCall to the continuation FnIr.

Parent: fz-ul4.11.15. Depends on: fz-ul4.11.15.2.

## Deliverables (extend src/ir_inline.rs)

  /// One pass over the module. For each fn C, for each block B with a
  /// Call(F, args) -> Cont{K, captured} terminator where F.id != C.id and
  /// is_inlinable(F), rewrite via splice. Returns total rewrites performed.
  pub fn inline_calls_once(m: &mut Module) -> usize;

## Rewrite recipe

  Given: caller C, block B with terminator `Call { callee: F, args,
  continuation: Cont { fn_id: K, captured } }`. F, K, C all in module m.

  1. Preconditions: F.id != C.id; is_inlinable(F).
  2. (F', vmap, bmap) = alpha_rename(F, &C).
  3. Walk F'.blocks; rewrite each terminator:
       Return(v')  ->  TailCall { callee: K, args: [v', captured...] }
                       The captured list comes from B's ORIGINAL Cont and uses
                       caller-local Var ids (not remapped by alpha_rename).
       Halt(v')    ->  unchanged.
       Goto/If     ->  unchanged.
       Other Term variants CANNOT appear — F is leaf.
  4. F'_entry = splice_blocks(&mut C, F').
  5. B's terminator becomes `Goto(F'_entry, args)`.

## Why TailCall(K, [v, captured...]) is semantically equivalent

  ir_interp.rs:231 (Term::Call semantics):
      let result = run_fn(module, *callee, arg_vals)?;
      let cont_args = [result] ++ collect(&env, &continuation.captured)?;
      fn_id = continuation.fn_id; args = cont_args; continue 'tail;

  Our rewrite produces TailCall(K, [v', captured...]). K's entry params are
  [result, cap_params...] (set up by cps_split_call in ir_lower.rs:1039).
  Identical operational result. The cont-frame alloc from emit_call
  (ir_codegen.rs:4680) is bypassed — fewer allocations.

## Safety w.r.t. cont_fns analysis

  codegen assigns cont-fn ABI (`(result, self) tail`) to every fn appearing
  in any Cont.fn_id field (ir_codegen.rs:1901-1917). The inliner rewrites
  the Call term, removing K from the cont position. This is safe because:

  - cps_split_call (ir_lower.rs:1039) mints a FRESH FnId for K per call site.
  - K appears in exactly ONE Term::Call.continuation.fn_id across the module.
  - After inlining, K has zero cont_fn references -> gets regular native sig.
  - K's entry params [result, cap_params...] match the TailCall args [v', captured...].
  - No ABI mismatch. K is now a plain TailCall target.

  If K is itself leaf and small, the fixpoint loop will inline K too.

## Captured-var lifetime

  B's original Cont.captured vars were defined in C before block B and remain
  live at the splice-injected Goto(F'_entry, args). They are still live at the
  rewritten TailCall(K, [v', captured]) because F' uses only fresh Vars (alpha
  rename shifted past C's max), so no new definitions interpose.

## Module.fn_idx note

  Module.fn_idx maps FnId -> index in module.fns. The inliner does not add
  new FnIrs, so fn_idx is never stale. When mutating caller C, obtain the
  callee's FnIr first (e.g. clone it), then borrow C mutably — Rust borrow
  rules require the immutable callee reference is not held across the mutation.

## Tests

  - Hand-built: `inc(x) = x + 1` (leaf, 2 stmts). Caller with
    `Call(inc, [x]) -> Cont{K, [y]}`. After pass: caller's term =
    `Goto(spliced_entry, [x])`; spliced inc-body computes `x+1`; spliced
    Return becomes `TailCall(K, [sum, y])`.
  - Self-recursion: not inlined.
  - Non-leaf: not inlined.
  - Over-budget: not inlined.
  - ir_interp parity: pre- and post-inline produce same Value sequence.
  - K is no longer in any Call.continuation after inlining (validate by
    scanning module.fns).

## Out of scope

  - Pipeline integration (.15.5 wires this in).

## Acceptance

  - cargo test passes.
  - One commit referencing fz-ul4.11.15.3.

## References

  - fz-ul4.11.15 architecture section, Case 2 and cont_fns safety.
  - ir_interp.rs:231 (Term::Call branch semantics).
  - ir_lower.rs:1039 (cps_split_call; K is single-site by construction).
  - ir_codegen.rs:1901-1917 (cont_fns analysis that inlining interacts with).
  - ir_codegen.rs:4680 (emit_call; bypassed after inlining).
title: Inline Call+Cont sites (Case 2)
